metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_928
metadata.comment:
Rationale: This study provides evidence for a role of CtBP1 in presynapse to nucleus signaling. CtBP1 was previously shown to act as a transcriptional repressor.

The authors overexpress CtBP1 coupled to photo-activatable GFP (CtBP1intPAEGFP), and demonstrate that photoactivation at the presynapse leads to a buildup of fluorescence in the nucleus (Figure 3). In line with a role in gene expression, knockdown of CtBP1 affects the expression of multiple neuronal genes (figure 5).. Experimental description: We used CtBP1-S (NM_019201.3, NP_062074.2) for all expression studies in this work.
(NP_062074.2 is rat protein, NCBI genpept)

24/11/2017 Pim:
Authors use a mix of rat and mouse tissues for their experiments. CTB1 overexpression constructs are rat species. Ctb1 location is primarily studied in rat tissue. Functions of Bsn and Pclo on Ctb1 are studied in a mouse context (KO animals).
Fig.3 uses overexpressed rat CtBP1 and therefore annotation is made against rat species.
